LAW AND MOTION 302, SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY'S DEMURRER TO THE SOLE CAUSE OF ACTION IN THE COMPLAINT FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT IS SUSTAINED WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF DONISE MANCHESTER'S COMPLAINT DOES NOT ADEQUATELY ALLEGE EITHER A BREACH OF CONTRACT CLAIM OR A DISABILITY DISCRIMINATION CLAIM. IF SHE CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, MS. MANCHESTER IS GIVEN LEAVE TO FILE A FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT ALLEGING EITHER OR BOTH A BREACH OF CONTRACT CLAIM BASED ON THE BREACH OF A SPECIFIC CONTRACT TERM OR A DISABILITY DISCRIMINATION CLAIM BASED ON THE FAILURE TO ACCOMMODATE HER DISABILITY. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

LAW AND MOTION, 302, SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY'S MOTION TO STRIKE THE ATTORNEY'S FEES ALLEGATIONS IN THE COMPLAINT IS OFF CALENDAR AS MOOT IN LIGHT OF THE RULING ON THE SFHA'S DEMURRER. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFFS SHALL AMEND THE COMPLAINT ON OR BEFORE FEBRUARY 14, 2017. WHILE NOTING THAT PLAINTIFF IS PRO PER AND SHOULD BE AFFORDED ANOTHER OPPORTUNITY TO STATE CLAIMS, THE COURT ALSO OBSERVES THAT THE AMENDMENT PROCESS CANNOT CONTINUE INDEFINITELY. THE COURT URGES PLAINTIFF TO CAREFULLY REVIEW PAGES 3 AND 4 OF THE MEMORANDUM DEFENDANT FILED ON JANUARY 6, 2017, WHICH SETS OUT DEFICIENCIES IN HER PLEADING. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR.; CLERK: FELICIA GREEN; COURT REPORTER: REYES HUNTER, CSR NO. 6576; DEPT. 302.

DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY'S MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFFS RESPONSES TO DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND SPECIALLY PREPARED INTERROGATORIES AND TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO DEFENDANTS FORM INTERROGATORIES GENERAL AND FORM INTERROGATORIES EMPLOYMENT LAW, AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST PLAINTIFF IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF IN HER INITIAL RESPONSES FAILED TO COMPLY WITH THE REQUIREMENTS OF C.C.P. SECTIONS 2030.210 AND 2031.210 REGARDING THE PROPER FORM AND FORMAT OF DISCOVERY RESPONSES. THE DEFENDANT IS ENTITLED TO VERIFIED RESPONSES IN THE FORM PRESCRIBED BY LAW. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. HOWEVER A FUTURE FAILURE TO FOLLOW THE DIRECTIVES OF THE CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE AND PROVIDE CODE COMPLAINT DISCOVERY RESPONSES MAY RESULT IN THE IMPOSITION OF MONETARY SANCTIONS AT THE DISCRETION OF THE COURT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE PRO TEM: STEVEN STEIN; CLERK: JOHNNY SENGMANY; NOT REPORTED.=302/JPT.

REQUEST PENDING TO WAIVE COURT FEES AND COSTS PURSUANT TO G.C. 68633, CRC 3.51, 8.26, AND 8.818
Off Calendar Aug-30-2017 Text Ruling

MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-11-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Plaintiff Donise Manchester's motion to strike the answer to the second amended complaint is denied. Ms. Manchester's motion impermissibly relies on facts not alleged in the answer or any of the affirmative defenses therein and thus is not a proper pleading motion. On a motion to strike the court is required to accept the truth of the factual allegations in the pleading that is challenged and may not consider facts not contained in the challenged pleading. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen CSR #8189 =302/hk

OBJECTION HEARING CALENDAR
Off Calendar Apr-19-2017 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Responses To Defendants Request For Production Of Documents And Specially Prepared Interrogatories And To Compel Further Responses To Defendants Form Interrogatories General And Form Interrogatories Employment Law, And For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff
Off Calendar Mar-07-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: PLAINTIFF DONISE MANCHESTER TEL NO. 707-712-2842 APPEARING IN PRO PER;
ATTY COLIN H. JEWELL OF CHOLAKIAN AND ASSOCIATES, PC. TEL NO. 650-871-9544 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING- A R G U E D, DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY's Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Responses To Defendant's Request For Production Of Documents And Specially Prepared Interrogatories And To Compel Further Responses To Defendants Form Interrogatories General And Form Interrogatories Employment Law, And For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff is granted. Plaintiff in her initial responses failed to comply with the requirements of C.C.P. sections 2030.210 and 2031.210 regarding the proper form and format of discovery responses. The defendant is entitled to verified responses in the form prescribed by law. Defendant's request for sanctions is denied. However a future failure to follow the directives of the Code of Civil Procedure and provide code complaint discovery responses may result in the imposition of monetary sanctions at the discretion of the Court. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge Pro tem: Steven Stein; Clerk: Johnny Sengmany; Not Reported.=302/JPT.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-01-2017 Continued To Apr-19-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-01-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Donis Manchester, in pro per, Tel. 707.712.2842, for plaintiffs;
Colin H. Jewell, of Law Offices of Cholakian & Associates, Tel. 650.871.9544, for defendants.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. Plaintiffs shall amend the complaint on or before February 14, 2017. While noting that plaintiff is pro per and should be afforded another opportunity to state claims, the Court also observes that the amendment process cannot continue indefinitely. The Court urges plaintiff to carefully review pages 3 and 4 of the memorandum defendant filed on January 6, 2017, which sets out deficiencies in her pleading. Order signed in open court. Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Reyes Hunter, CSR No. 6576; Dept. 302.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-29-2016 Continued To Feb-15-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-24-2016 Continued To Dec-14-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-27-2016 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Plaintiff Donise Manchester telephone number 707-712-2842 in Propria Persona.
Colin Jewell of Cholakian & Associates telephone number 650-871-9544 for Defendant San Francisco Housing Authority.

The Court explained the tentative ruling to Ms. Manchester. Upon conclusion, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling. San Francisco Housing Authority's demurrer to the sole cause of action in the complaint for breach of contract is sustained with twenty days leave to amend. Plaintiff Donise Manchester's complaint does not adequately allege either a breach of contract claim or a disability discrimination claim. If she can do so in good faith, Ms. Manchester is given leave to file a first amended complaint alleging either or both a breach of contract claim based on the breach of a specific contract term or a disability discrimination claim based on the failure to accommodate her disability. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)
